name: A New Adventure
description: A classic Myrdae campaign built around exploration, table discovery, and the next generation of heroes stepping into a changed world.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 28 - Caelora has Issues!
sessionSummaries.summary: The party continued to help Affy follow a lead in Basctdelm and help Ridley with business in Caelora. At the Whisperpine campsite they encountered the moontouched half-orc adventurer Kulv Farbreak, who hinted at powers echoing Fungus's own, while back at the fire a spirit of an elder druid from Iparos possessed Relys with a command to avenge her village. Upon reaching the terraced city, Urovil Rulendor greeted Ridley with news of an Airithel misfortune. Cerul sensed something foul trailing from the towards House Rulendor and Kaizo's earnest herb inquiries at Dawnpetal went sideways. Nanise Rulendor intercepted Ridley and abruptly relieved him of his duties.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 27 - Don't Threaten Us
sessionSummaries.summary: The caravan was confronted by an elvish wizard and a burly warrior demanding the prisoners' release. The fight ended with the attackers dead, but Ridley's long-sought robed elf escaped during the chaos.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 26 - To Caelora and Beyond
sessionSummaries.summary: The party escorted wagons toward Shademoor, uncovered one of Ridley's targets among the prisoners, gained a bulas named Trygg, and defeated an oni and trolls at a stone bridge.

audioLinks.label: Session 26 - To Caelora and Beyond Recording

sessionSummaries.title: Session 25 - To Qal'dynn
sessionSummaries.summary: Everyone exited the Blackstone Crucible with the abacus after testing the gate. On the way to the Abbey, they met spectral giant Amag who warned Cerul of an unresolved scar beneath his birthplace in destroyed Pekanon. Kaizo continued to have difficulty meditating, hinting that Malgri'n was still an issue. They took the Arcane Waypoint to Qal'dynn and met with Lorain Ormseer. Gaining clues about Affy's father and the Promisory in Basctdelm, they agreed to pick up a job as guards for a few wagons that headed that way. Ridley did have some unfinished matters with a clue he uncovered on his hunt for Heildove.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 24 - Into the Shadowfell
sessionSummaries.summary: The party set out to investigate and seal the dangerous rift between the material plane and the Shadowfell near Varenwood, and learn what they could of the ruined gnome village of Iparos. After receiving vital information from Zaedra Windfury of the Everdawn, they recovered faint magical remnants from the ruined settlement before stepping through the rift. In the Shadowfell, waves of shadowy assailants and an avatar of Malgri'n assailed them, nearly claiming Fungus before the group rallied before defeated the avatar. Tananan appeared and carried the Feywoven Glyphic away. The party returned to the material plane and dispelled the rift's anchoring magic.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 23 - Their Fates Tied
sessionSummaries.summary: The party sought to understand the true purpose of the Blackstone Crucible and determine whether the powerful being bound within it should remain imprisoned. After encountering a Shadar-kai visitor investigating yugoloth activity and learning that Kael Vorduun had been bound as an example by Malgri'n, the party made a fateful oath to release him in exchange for binding another deemed worthy in the future. The session ended with the group stepping through the portal into Varenwood, realizing that Kaizo's disrupted balance, Fungus's patron's imprisonment, and Relys's ruined village were all tied together by the same force.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 22 - The Blackstone Crucible
sessionSummaries.summary: The party entered a newly revealed black stone structure with the goal of understanding its purpose. Inside, they discovered it's name, the Blackstone Crucible, a vast and ancient construct devoted to the binding and containment of dangerous forces, guided by a rigid doctrine that framed imprisonment as mercy. Their exploration uncovered ritual chambers, indoctrination texts, and an encounter with extra-planar fiends. In the aftermath, the party discovered a mysterious gate calibrated to distant locations and encountered a strange counting entity that reported their presence to a figure named Varmuun.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 21 - The Emerging Temple
sessionSummaries.summary: The party set out to investigate the deadly cave route near the Abbey, hoping to learn why no one returned and whether it connected to the wider threats they had been uncovering. Inside, they found signs of curse driven deaths and recovered evidence tying the site back to Dunduar and the strange forces already in motion. Their caution paid off when they defeated a corrupted stone giant guarding the path beyond the ravine. The session ended with a major revelation as the party emerged to find a massive black stone temple newly risen from the earth, its sealed doors marked with an ancient oath to a long forgotten power.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 20 - Restoring the Abbey
sessionSummaries.summary: The party worked to secure the Abbey now that its arcane waypoint was restored. They confronted a powerful infiltrator, the Parson, who revealed himself by turning allies to stone, but through coordinated effort and timely magic, the party defeated him and reversed the petrification. In the aftermath, they uncovered hidden vaults and sealed Underdark access beneath the Abbey, choosing restraint over greed to protect what had been entrusted to the order. The session closed with the Abbey finally safe, its people openly grateful, and the party recognized for having turned a looming collapse into a moment of renewed hope.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 19 - Ham's Helping Hand
sessionSummaries.summary: The party pressed deeper beneath the Abbey with the goal of dismantling the Rel'Udomina cell. They exposed another operative group, confirmed that the conspiracy relied on ordinary members of the Abbey above ground, and learned the Rel'Udomina were structured into multiple Looms overseen by Archons. Pushing onward, they fought through possessed defenders and wraithlike entities guarding the waypoint chamber, breaking the possession and reactivating the portal. The session ended the moment the restored waypoint drew hostile attention, leaving the party face to face with a furious new threat just as help could finally reach the Abbey.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 18 - Abbey Undercroft
sessionSummaries.summary: The party worked to uncover how deeply the Rel'Udomina had infiltrated the Abbey and to locate the source of the threats spreading beneath its halls. Their investigation confirmed the existence of hidden passages and planted arcane devices meant to isolate the Abbey from outside aid, forcing them to descend into the undercroft. There, they exposed an active Rel'Udomina cell operating inside the Abbey itself and fought to prevent its members from escaping deeper underground. The session ended with prisoners freed, documents recovered, and the grim realization that trusted figures within the Abbey had been compromised as part of a wider coordinated conspiracy.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 17 - Abbey Arrival
sessionSummaries.summary: The group aimed to reach the Abbey, survive the ambush that had petrified a stone giant ally, and find a way inside to learn what threat had taken hold. They fought off a coordinated attack of stone and winged predators, then attempted to restore their ally with powerful magic, only to see it fail. With no cure in hand, Cerul used his history and voice among the stone giants to negotiate passage, earning a guarded escort into the Abbey grounds. Once inside, the party learned the unsettling truth, something was moving beneath the Abbey, and fear, silence, and hostage threats had kept everyone trapped in place.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 16 - To the Abbey
sessionSummaries.summary: The party set out from Qal'dynn with the urgent goal of reaching the Abbey, uncovering what had gone wrong there, and finding a way to save a stone giant ally who had been turned to stone. Along the way, they gathered limited but critical resources, learned that the threat surrounding the Abbey was as spiritual as it was physical, and received troubling warnings tied to fate and borrowed power. As they neared their destination, signs of devastation mounted, shattered caravans, giant carved warnings, and a looming presence in the distance. The session ended the moment the party found the petrified giant and was immediately ambushed.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 15 - Empower the Charm
sessionSummaries.summary: The party sought to empowering Fungus's fey charm and securing long term stability for their growing influence in Qal'dynn. Through alchemical work and a moonlit ritual at the Ring of Sylunara, they successfully awakened the Charm of the Slumbering Grove, only to draw immediate attention from hostile fey forces seeking to reclaim it. A sudden, intense clash ended with the attackers defeated, valuable spoils recovered, and the realization that powerful fey lords were now aware of the party and their actions. In the aftermath, the party finalized legal control of their land, set plans in motion for a retreat centered on balance and harmony, and regrouped as Cerul returned, marking a moment of consolidation before the next looming test.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 14 - Thinbrew Hideaway
sessionSummaries.summary: The party set out to secure rare Lusterbee wax for Iofyr's alchemical work while confirming whether the Rel'Udomina were still operating in the region. Their search led them to a hidden ravine operation where a stolen queen bee was being exploited by a black market group guarded by goblins and a dangerous mage. Through careful timing and decisive combat, the party dismantled the operation, recovered the queen and hive, and prevented the alarm from spreading. They returned the queen safely and earned the wax they needed, closing the session with a critical resource secured and warnings of greater danger still looming at the Abbey.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 13 - Qal'dynn Mysteries
sessionSummaries.summary: The party focused on following new leads within Qal'dynn. Their investigation uncovered a murdered messenger carrying a warning meant for Affy, pointing toward Basctdelm and her father, and revealed that someone was deliberately targeting those connected to her past. At the same time, they confronted unresolved threats beneath Moonroot Hollow, destroying arcane parasites and stabilizing a damaged leypost before the situation could worsen. The session closed with a sobering realization that the Rel'Udomina were still active in the city, even after everything the party had exposed.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 12 - Ambassador Hamalex
sessionSummaries.summary: The party sought to solidify their standing in Qal'dynn by turning their discoveries into lasting action and helping stabilize the dangers threatening the city. They earned the king's trust, pursued solutions to lingering magical threats, and chose diplomacy over force when unrest flared in Moonroot Hollow, preventing further damage to the ley thread beneath the city. Their efforts culminated in a measured, peaceful resolution that protected both the city and those caught in the conflict. By the end of the session, Ham was formally named Ambassador of Qal'dynn, marking a turning point where the party's influence shifted from covert intervention to recognized authority.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 11 - Audience with the King
sessionSummaries.summary: The party worked to escape the Rel'Udomina tunnels beneath Qal'dynn, secure official authority, and finally deliver their evidence and sealed message to the crown. After fighting through infiltrators and exposing assassins posing as realmwardens within the university, they seized proof that the conspiracy had reached deep into the city itself. They regrouped long enough to manage the cube's dangerous side effects and coordinate a lawful approach to power rather than continuing as fugitives. The session culminated in a formal escort into the citadel, where the party was granted an audience with King Highvale.

audioLinks.label: Session 11 - Audience with the King Recording

sessionSummaries.title: Session 10 - Prominent Rel'Udomina
sessionSummaries.summary: The party pressed deeper into the Rel'Udomina hideout beneath Qal'dynn with the goal of confronting its leadership, dismantling the operation, and securing a path to warn the king. They defeated the organization's prominent leader after a hard fought clash, uncovering evidence of covert communication, fey involvement, and a network designed to spy on and destabilize the city. The session ended in rising tension as an attempted escape, the sound of fighting above, and the sudden arrival of a Realmwarden made it clear that their secret war beneath Qal'dynn was about to become very public.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 9 - Rel'Udomina Hideout
sessionSummaries.summary: The group entered the hidden tunnels beneath Qal'dynn with the goal of exposing the Rel'Udomina's operations and protecting the sealed message they carried. As they explored the hideout, they uncovered incriminating maps and documents pointing to a figure known as The Prominent, confirmed the tunnels were being used to spy directly on the king, and seized a powerful scrying device as proof. When deception failed, the party eliminated a cell of Rel'Udomina agents and identified their mark, then forced a hired guard to stand down and reveal how the organization moved through the city unseen. The session ended on a sharp cliffhanger as the party opened a final chamber and came face to face with a woman who knew Affy by name, flanked by hounds and something far more dangerous.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 8 - The Secret Path Into Qal'dynn
sessionSummaries.summary: The party pressed toward Qal'dynn with the urgent goal of delivering the sealed scroll and uncovering the truth behind the Rel'Udomina, only to find the city gates firmly closed. After failing to gain entry, they followed a hidden route marked on their map, using a spoken key to reveal a secret passage into the earth beneath the city. Within the tunnels, they overcame a powerful construct guarding an ancient door tied to the Aetherion Academy. The party unlocked the passage and stood on the threshold of Qal'dynn at last, carrying both proof of conspiracy and the weight of what awaited inside.

audioLinks.label: Session 8 - The Secret Path Into Qal'dynn Recording

sessionSummaries.title: Session 7 - Elle'berian Shrine
sessionSummaries.summary: Our adventurers returned to O'naren to secure official support and follow leads tied to the Rel'Udomina. In town, they recovered a pendant linked to Affy's past, planted the seeds of a quiet sanctuary through Kaizo's efforts, and accepted a formal mission to find a missing courier after learning Qal'dynn had sealed its gates. On the road, the cube's influence deepened, granting Fungus a lasting boon while afflicting Affy with a disturbing transformation. The session culminated at a defiled Elle'berian shrine, where the party exposed and defeated disguised Rel'Udomina agents, uncovering orders to assassinate a diplomat and the grim fate of the courier they were sent to find.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 6 - Cackletongue Lair
sessionSummaries.summary: Our adventurers pushed into the gnoll lair to eliminate the source of the raids and uncover what had corrupted the Silent Circle. Inside the caves, they battled spore filled fungal growths, survived a deadly ambush by an ooze, and fought through a smoke choked chamber to bring down the gnoll pack leader and his infernal ally. Along the way they uncovered valuable loot and disturbing signs that something far darker than gnolls had taken root in the region. The session ended with a shocking revelation as Cerul manifested radiant wings, confirming the meaning behind the taunts of 'angel blood' and reshaping how the party understood one of their own.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 5 - Silent Circle Gnolls
sessionSummaries.summary: The group worked to secure allies in O'naren, uncover the truth behind the growing threat tied to the Rel'Udomina, and end the gnoll raids defiling the Silent Circle before the corruption spread further. They sought insight from the Oracle, who confirmed that answers about the strange essence powder and Fungus's mysterious artifact lay with Iofyr in Qal'dynn. That night, the party confronted the gnolls at the Silent Circle and discovered the satyr druid Kasani had been overtaken by a malignant influence. After a brutal fight that destroyed the gnolls and the wailing fungal growth empowering them, the party salvaged what remained of the sacred site and followed the attackers' trail to a nearby cave. There, blue smoke and a chilling voice waiting within made it clear that the danger was far from over.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 4 - In O'naren
sessionSummaries.summary: Our adventurers arrived in O'naren intent on uncovering who was behind the killings on the road, finding answers about the strange powder afflicting Ro's father, and warning local authorities about the growing threats tied to the Broken Citadel. As they investigated the town, they exposed a middleman named Kool'ok and traced the violence back to Vrathin, the local mapmaker, while also learning the powder may be an essence substance capable of anchoring souls between worlds. Their search led them to new dangers and obligations, including nightly gnoll raids on a sacred druidic site and deeper ties to the Cloak of Defiance than first assumed. By the end of the session, discussions with Captain Salal revealed the situation was far more dire, pointing not to common criminal intrigue, but to the shadow of Rel'Udomina itself.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 3 - Road to O'naren
sessionSummaries.summary: The party set out from Mossholde toward O'naren, aiming to have the strange powder examined and to warn others about the dangers uncovered beneath the Broken Citadel. Along the road, they experimented further with the rune covered cube, triggering unpredictable effects creating suspicions of the artifact's far reaching consequences. Their journey was marked by violence, including an ambush by strange creatures and a desperate chase against hired killers preying on fleeing civilians. Although the party defeated the attackers and gathered evidence of a larger plot, they arrived in O'naren carrying the heavy cost of lives they could not save.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 2 - Beneath the Citadel
sessionSummaries.summary: The party focused on uncovering what lay beneath the Broken Citadel. After splitting briefly to ensure the rescued wolf pup and the strange powder were returned safely to Mossholde, the remaining adventurers descended into the ruins below and found evidence of layered, ancient uses, including alchemical work, undead guardians, and infernal summoning. Through several tense fights and careful exploration, they uncovered a long forgotten teleportation circle, a sealed Feywild portal, and a rune covered cube pulsing with unstable magic. By the end of the delve, the group realized the citadel was a crossroads of powerful forces, and that answers for Ro's father and warnings for Cerul's allies both pointed them toward Qal'dynn as their next destination.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 1 - Met in Mossholde
sessionSummaries.summary: In the first session, the party came together at the remote outpost of Mossholde, each carrying their own reasons for being on the frontier. A badly wounded elf staggered in during the night, he warned them of an ambush by strange blue creatures and pointed toward the ruins of an old citadel in the southern valley. Choosing action over caution, the group tracked the attackers at first light, uncovering signs of grisly feeding and unnatural behavior. Their pursuit led them into the Broken Citadel itself, where a hard fought battle revealed organized enemies, unsettling magical clues, and the first clear link between the ruins and the poison that had sickened Ro's father.

name: Bloody Endeavor
description: A darker campaign of hard choices, blood-soaked consequences, and morally tangled stories in the rougher corners of Myrdae.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 35 - Bloody Endeavor: Civil War
sessionSummaries.summary: Wyrm Bane entered the Ring of Respect, defeated the Sacred Crows, gained access to Khagdaruhel, researched the city's lords, and prepared for an imperial audience.

audioLinks.label: Session 35 - Bloody Endeavor: Civil War Recording

sessionSummaries.title: Session 34 - Greatest of All Time
sessionSummaries.summary: The group recovered the blind goat, cleared drow-held towers, reached Khagdaruhel, and began the Ring of Respect challenges.

audioLinks.label: Session 34 - Greatest of All Time Recording

sessionSummaries.title: Session 33 - Caw!
sessionSummaries.summary: Descended from the Vanapur Mountains toward Khagdaruhel, the party were still commited to geting the dwarves support and they wanted to investigate rumors of the Cold Breath's growing influence on the Rimestone. Along the way they encountered the Sacred Crows, a capable adventuring group, who had been stalled outside Khagdaruhel searching for a missing blind goat beloved by a dwarven knight. The two parties formed a temporary alliance, tracked the goat to a foul cave occupied by an elder ettin. Dispatching the giant, they pressed deeper to discover ancient dwarven towers now held by drow bearing banners of fangs and webs. In a coordinated assault on the towers, the combined force battled through the drow and a venomous spider-like boss that webbed and struck from the shadows.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 32 - Over the Edge
sessionSummaries.summary: The group was in a dwarven tomb the Vanapur Mountains that have been claimed by Presagiath. After battling a fiery copper dwarven construct guardian in an ancient tomb and climbing a hidden spiral staircase into her frozen cavern, they were ambushed by the dragon, who had been clinging unseen to the ceiling. A brutal fight unfolded atop a sheer cliff edge, with Presagiath's chilling breath, grapples, and near-fatal drops nearly wiping the group; several members. Pagern delivered the killing firebolt that sent the dragon plummeting to her death, allowing the battered survivors to claim a vast hoard of coin, gems, art objects, and powerful magic items.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 31 - Don't Let This Drag-on
sessionSummaries.summary: It was time to follow a dwarven map into the Vanapur Mountains in search of a hidden treasure trove that could fund their future ambitions. Their exploration uncovered a sealed dwarven fortress haunted by the legacy of Mornok the Flayer, where constructs, traps, and restless guardians still enforced ancient defenses. As they pushed deeper, the danger escalated with a brutal clash against a white dragon Presagiath that nearly claimed multiple lives. The session ended when the party breached the inner tomb, triggering hostile wards that divided them and leaving the true cost of disturbing the fortress yet to be revealed.

audioLinks.label: Session 31 - Don't Let This Drag-on Recording

sessionSummaries.title: Session 30 - Let's Break A Deal!
sessionSummaries.summary: The party set out to confront the consequences of their bargain with the hags and decide whether to uphold or break the deal entirely. The situation escalated when a warning vision revealed the growing threat of Coldbreath, after which the party turned on their hag escort and fought to end the pact by force. The battle was brutal and costly, ending with the hag's death but leaving Lucerion maimed when he lost an arm to corrosive magic. The party then faced Madam Mabel, completed the exchange on altered terms, and watched her restore her youth, before regrouping and moving on under the weight of new power, new scars, and far-reaching consequences.

audioLinks.label: Session 30 - Let's Break A Deal! Recording

sessionSummaries.title: Session 29 - Hags with Bags
sessionSummaries.summary: The party set out to repay a dangerous favor owed to Madam Mabel by stepping through a Fey-touched gateway and retrieving specific items from the strange locals beyond it. Their journey quickly escalated, forcing them to fight off an orc warband, navigate a hostile and watchful mine, and confront warped fey creatures. The task culminated in a brutal clash with three disguised hags, a fight that drained the party but revealed the true nature of their opposition. Rather than ending in simple victory, the session closed with a tense negotiation with a more powerful hag, leaving the party bound by a precarious bargain and facing far more complicated consequences.

name: Collective of Myrdae
description: A pickup-group pool for players to keep characters ready for smaller adventures and shared sessions across the wider table.

sessionSummaries.title: Pickup Character Pool
sessionSummaries.summary: This is not an official campaign. It is a place for players to keep characters ready for pickup groups or smaller session groups using D&D 2024 rules.

name: Dungeons III
description: A dungeon-forward adventure with inventive encounters, table momentum, and old-school discovery in the world of Myrdae.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 11 - Sand People Get In All the Crevices
sessionSummaries.summary: The kNight Watch pressed deeper into the Jagged Waste Crags to locate the Radiant Eye cult outpost and secure the lost celestial instrument. They defeated a band of Mahruudian Raiders in a fierce running battle across the cliffs, claiming two kahlbits as new mounts along with supplies and a potion of fire breath. Seraphine examined the raiders' strange sun-sensitive bodies before the party fought and killed two packs of dire wolves that had been feeding on humanoids. They tracked the wolves to a series of terraced caves, avoided a hidden pit trap under a blood-soaked tapestry at the entrance, and the session ended with the group standing in a suspicious cave.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 10 - It's a Long Ass Way!
sessionSummaries.summary: The kNight Watch rode out from Scarwatch Hold toward the eastern crags to investigate the Radiant Eye cult outpost and secure a lost celestial instrument while pursuing Isgo's kidnappers. They discovered Isgo's horse Bullseye and learned that two unknown riders had taken him, then endured several days of difficult travel through increasingly craggy terrain. Along the way they found a hidden cache of gold and potions, discovered a decomposed corpse, and defeated a Xorn that erupted from the earth. The session ended as Meles scouted ahead and spotted two figures in tan robes, who attacked the party as they approached.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 9 - A Cart-time Job
sessionSummaries.summary: In Scarwatch Hold, the kNight Watch received their assignment from Warden Haliun to hunt Isgo. Umlin commissioned the party to complete a map of the eastern lands for 20 gold pieces. Shopping and tavern time followed, including reunions with Dempsey and Meles. The next morning the Radiant Eye cult symbol appeared on the Sanctuary wall, shifting priorities. Nambra redirected them to investigate the cult outpost in the eastern crags and secure a lost celestial instrument from nearby ruins. The party rode out mid-morning toward the crags, crossing the drawbridge as a lone black horse with a white blaze appeared riderless in the distance.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 8 - Mimic at the Gate
sessionSummaries.summary: In Scarwatch Hold, the party was wrapping up local personal matters and responsibilities around the K9 Crew prisoners. A smoking wagon arrived and a mimic disguised as a barrel attacked with acidic tendrils. The adventurers fought it off fiercely. Nixie and Aeon delivered the prisoners' charges to Warden Naevaris Haliun, who confirmed the judgment for the next day. That night a shadowy burglar was driven from the bank. The following morning the Cap'n confessed dealings with the Scorpion as Draelith messed with the sending stone. A mysterious voice replied that their interference had complicated matters yet perhaps the party would be useful.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 7 - Brooch the Subject
sessionSummaries.summary: The party's primary goal was to escort the captured K9 Crew bandits, including their wounded leader Captain Kinrik, from Hesfal to Scarwatch Hold and personally deliver them along with the sealed charges directly to Warden Haliun in the citadel. On the road, they encountered the abrasive half-orc Isgo, who recognized the crew; after some taunting from Draelith and intimidation by Nova, he backed off, though Meles later confirmed the Blackshield family smiths had clashed with him earlier and emerged unharmed. The group reached Scarwatch Hold without major incident, secured lodging, gathered rumors at the tavern, and the next morning pressed forward to complete the handover, only for a massive explosion to erupt at the Outer Gates, ending the session on that sudden, chaotic note.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 6 - Rope A Dope
sessionSummaries.summary: The party focused on securing their prisoners and uncovering the true scope of the plot behind the chaos in Hesfal. They learned that the bandits were being directed through an amplified sending stone embedded in a magically attuned cavern, confirming that the Scorpion had planned more than a simple robbery and jailbreak. While investigating the depths of the hideout, the party fought and defeated dangerous creatures drawn to the cavern's magic, revealing a potential path toward the Underdark below. After returning the bandits to Hesfal and agreeing to transport them to Scarwatch Hold, the session ended with a final shock as an unknown figure was discovered rifling through Nova's belongings, signaling that the danger was far from over.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 5 - The Hots for Nova
sessionSummaries.summary: An ambush was set up to catch the escaped bandits and uncover who had orchestrated the chaos in Hesfal. Their ambush indirectly paid off, leading them from the bandits' horse drop through dangerous terrain to a hidden cave where they decisively dismantled the crew and forced their surrender. Interrogation revealed that the robbery, jailbreak, and murder arrest were all part of a paid plan meant to spread fear during Salvus, ordered by an unknown figure called the Scorpion. Everything came to an end with the chilling realization that their unseen employer was still watching, as a disembodied voice questioned the failure of the plan.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 4 - I Can't Even Take A ...
sessionSummaries.summary: The party set out to track down the escaped bandits and the freed prisoner before they could disappear beyond Hesfal's reach. Following the trail into dense wilderness, they uncovered signs of deception, fought through hostile terrain, and survived a violent encounter with an owlbear and a swarm of stirges that nearly cost them their mounts. Their pursuit paid off with the discovery of a hidden cache and the bandits' horses, confirming they were closing in. The session ended with the party setting an ambush and holding their ground as the sound of someone approaching broke the silence.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 3 - Chase the Runners
sessionSummaries.summary: The party focused on containing the fallout from the coordinated attacks in Hesfal and preventing the perpetrators from escaping justice. They attempted to stop the fleeing criminals using quick thinking and improvised tools, but the escape only partially failed, leaving unanswered questions and wounded allies in its wake. As the town struggled to regain order, the group uncovered troubling signs that the robbery, jailbreak, and recent murder were connected. By morning, they committed to pursuing the runners beyond Hesfal, gathering evidence, securing mounts, and preparing to take the hunt onto the road.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 2 - A Pocket Prism
sessionSummaries.summary: The party tried to enjoy a brief return to normalcy as Hesfal filled with travelers ahead of the Salvus festival, while keeping a wary eye on rumors of violence and unrest. What began as music, conversation, and curiosity about strange newcomers quickly turned dangerous when a coordinated tavern robbery erupted around them. The group stepped in to protect civilians and stop the attackers, only to discover the violence was part of something larger when the Town Ward was breached and prisoners were freed. By the end of the night, the party found themselves split across multiple threats, realizing that the chaos in Hesfal was organized, deliberate, and far from over.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 1 - It's Aeon
sessionSummaries.summary: The party was united by circumstance, curiosity, and quiet personal searching as they each arrived in Hesfal on the eve of the Salvus festival. Individually, they sought rest, healing, faith, or simple opportunity, crossing paths in temples, taverns, and trading halls while sharing fragments of their pasts. Conversations revealed lingering scars from the Awakening, unresolved devotions to distant gods, and hints of deeper mysteries tying some of them together. By session's end, chance encounters gave way to recognition and shared history, just as the arrival of the final Salvus caravan signaled that Hesfal was about to become crowded, tense, and ripe for whatever fate was drawing them together.

name: Heroes of Emberstran
description: Heroes rise near volcanic Mount Emberstran, where dwarven holds, Bathaen wilds, and ancient danger meet.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 26 -- Bad News & A Wild Goose Chase
sessionSummaries.summary: Grim news came that Phira had been seized by the Cloak of Defiance and was being taken to the Shadow Sovereign in Basctdelm. They secured the silver dragon scale from Lady Rook, bartered for an arcane sigil to reach Basctdelm, uncovered troubling drow threads from Ys'nonterien, and sent the Arnol to safety while Diverra's guidance through Aury turned them away from direct confrontation with the Cloak and toward the one remaining threat in Emberstran. At the sealed cult hideout they faced a zombie beholder and a vampire who whispered of Countess Nas Verdidra while sinking her fangs into Ains and draining his vitality, until steel, daylight, and rage ended the creatures in fire and ash. The session closed with Zabeek's voice echoing through their fading telepathic bond that a mysterious stranger had just given him a ruby ring.

audioLinks.label: Session 26 -- Bad News & A Wild Goose Chase Recording

sessionSummaries.title: Session 25 -- Grief Stricken
sessionSummaries.summary: The party entered the Emberheart Vault, faced trials and a spectral guardian, met Irragosa, and returned to Emberstran carrying grim news from Ulgrey.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 24 -- Let the Bodies Hit the Floor
sessionSummaries.summary: The heroes followed the Emberheart Tablet through Mount Emberstran, survived a firenewt ambush, sheltered with rock gnomes, and opened a dark corridor ahead.

audioLinks.label: Session 24 -- Let the Bodies Hit the Floor Recording

sessionSummaries.title: Session 23 -- Into the Fire
sessionSummaries.summary: The party continued towards Mount Emberstran via the right-hand trail, seeking the source of the tablet's pull and deeper answers about the mountain's secrets, Irragosa, and the ancient journal's revelations. They encountered the bronze dragon Irragosa herself, who tasked Ky'tha with watching Ains, shared cryptic assurances, then departed. After camp reflections and minor combats with dire wolves and fire beetles, they reached the volcano's immense lava-filled heart, diplomatically appeased a towering flaming giant through tribute and sacrifice, then crested an embankment to witness a terrified gnome fleeing a warband of newt-like humanoids.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 22 - The Tracker
sessionSummaries.summary: The Heroes of Emberstran pressed along the Old Trader's Trail toward Mount Emberstran and the Emberheart Vault. They witnessed a bronze dragon rip a flying ship from the sky and forded a boiling river teeming with fire-scaled fish using ropes. There they exposed an invisible tracker, the wood elf Petal, who admitted serving Lady Rook to ensure their success. After a tense nighttime warning and her return at a trail fork the next morning, Zymve ended her with a swift dagger to the throat. They continued toward the mountain, now one day closer but carrying fresh doubts about their hidden patron.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 21 - He Ate a Baby!
sessionSummaries.summary: The heroes woke in the Temple of Diverra, still reeling from the Marrowstone ritual that left Ainslie suspiciously youthful. Their goal was to head north toward Mount Emberstran. Ky'tha reminded them of their promise to Lady Rook, which they managed a convenient excuse to delay. The party secured discounted supplies with their blessed badges, bought a goat and learned rumors of the dragon. Outside the gate the goat transformed into a sturdy gertot. A red rider delivered a threat from Jerik before Og and Ainslie struck him down, with Ainslie declaring the man 'ate a baby' to calm onlookers. They fought off quaraths on the Old Trader's Trail, then camped under the stars until a huge dragon's roar woke them at dawn as it soared toward a flying ship in the sky.

audioLinks.label: Session 21 - He Ate a Baby! Recording

sessionSummaries.title: Session 20 - Coming Home
sessionSummaries.summary: Returning to home, the party wanted to see how the city of Emberstran was holding up, find clues to get to the Emberheart Vault and bring their new companion Zymve into the community. They secured critical information from Lady Rook about combining the stone with Imonorra's rod to unleash the Marrowstone's necrotic power. After tense discussions about the moral risks of using the Marrowstone against the Cloak of Defiance, the group committed to the plan, thinking the rewards outweighed the risks. A hunter at the Warm Bear Inn provided key information about getting to Mount Emberstran to search for the Emberheart Vault. Things took an unexpected turn, when Ainslie was able to focus on the Marrowstone, extracting its deadly energy and leaving the stone empty.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 19 - Lost Sanctuary
sessionSummaries.summary: After pressing onward from the fungal tunnels of Thallgrove, the party followed their map marker through the Underdark with one main goal, to reach the Lost Sanctuary. They arrived at a blood-red lake, activated an ancient stone gate using Aury's divine power, and stepped through into a hidden surface grove. There they fought a fierce battle against corrupting blights, ultimately prevailing. In the aftermath, Og received a powerful calling as the thorn-bearer and guardian of the Alferumn with instructions to seek Dandelia of the Veil, while Aury gained the original Bloom Aeterna tome, leaving the group with renew purpose.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 18 - In Bimblefol
sessionSummaries.summary: The party had an escort through Thallgrove and a clue about the location of the Lost Sanctuary of Diverra. Following the genling, Makra, the party's trek was interrupted by two deadly Thall Mounds. Surviving a brutal fight that nearly claimed Og, the party carried two fallen genlings back to their hidden village. In Bimblefol, they gained the trust of the genlings, secured a fungal map marking the sanctuary's direction, and traded for water breathing supplies necessary for the journey ahead. By the end of the session, the party had clear direction, new provisions, and confirmation that reaching the sanctuary would require passing through deep water and deeper dangers.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 17 - Giant's Silver
sessionSummaries.summary: The party set out to earn additional favor in Nunglthil by dealing with stone giants who had seized a fishing hole on Lake Rothenloch. Negotiations failed, leading to a brutal fight that ended with both giants dead and the discovery of a rich silver ore deposit beneath the lake. The party harvested the silver, gained favor with multiple enclaves by restoring access to the lake, and sold the ore for coin and standing. They then secured passage across Rothenloch toward Thallgrove, where the lake's ominous presence made itself known and their journey ended with the discovery of dead adventurers and a cautious alliance with a genling guide.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 16 - Favor in Nunglthil
sessionSummaries.summary: After returning from the Spitespirit Cache with valuable gems, the party set out to convert their success into something that mattered in Nunglthil: visible favor. They secured their first favor beads from Coppereyes, learned how reputation functions as currency in the Underdark, and began positioning themselves with multiple enclaves. Along the way, they gathered crucial leads, including maps toward a lost Diverra sanctuary, confirmation that a drowned waypoint once existed beneath Lake Rothenloch, and new personal threads tied to Shadowtrail and a mysterious journal linked to Tsulanan. By the end of the session, the party had earned standing, opened several new paths forward, and accepted that every opportunity in Nunglthil comes with an attached obligation.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 15 - The Spitespirit Cache
sessionSummaries.summary: The party entered Nunglthil with the clear goal of earning favor in a city where reputation mattered more than coin. They accepted a dangerous job to recover the contents of the Spitespirit Cache, knowing success would establish them as capable and reliable outsiders. The expedition led them through lethal traps and into a sealed chamber where they were forced to fight a powerful guardian bound to the cache. Against the odds, the party prevailed, claimed the hidden treasure, and emerged with both valuable spoils and the opportunity to secure their first real standing within Nunglthil.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 14 - The Drowned Waypoint
sessionSummaries.summary: The party sought to escape the tightening political pressure of Emberstran and follow Diverra's hidden waypoint. The divine circle carried them far from the surface world, depositing them beneath the sea and into an ancient, long abandoned route. After navigating hostile creatures and treacherous terrain, the party emerged onto a vast underground lake and made contact with a ferryman who led them toward Nunglthil, an Underdark outpost where new dangers and opportunities awaited.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 13 -The Emberheart Tablet
sessionSummaries.summary: The party sought to assemble and understand the Emberheart Tablet while navigating growing pressure from Emberstran's shadow networks. Across town, the Thornleaf reacted strongly, binding more deeply to Og and hinting at a connection to the Alferumn. At the same time, Ky's ties to the Etterset sharpened, making it clear the group was now being watched, weighed, and quietly invested in by powers that thrived on leverage. When the tablet awakened and attuned to Aury, its message pointed unmistakably toward Mount Emberstran, confirming that the path forward would lead away from city politics and into something far older and more dangerous.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 12 - The Eradic of Ghone
sessionSummaries.summary: The party worked to stabilize their position in Emberstran while deciding how deeply to involve themselves with the city's competing power blocs. They gathered information from allies, questioned cult activity tied to Ghone, and tried to understand how far the Cloak and other shadowed forces had already spread. Og and Ainslie infiltrated the Eradic of Ghone under false pretenses, uncovering a hidden operation beneath the cemetery and securing a crucial tablet piece without triggering open conflict. By the end of the session, they had weakened the cult's position, completed the tablet set, and stepped more firmly into Emberstran's political and spiritual crosscurrents, knowing neutrality was becoming harder to maintain.

audioLinks.label: Session 12 - The Eradic of Ghone Recording

sessionSummaries.title: Session 11 - Confronting the Cload
sessionSummaries.summary: The party clashed directly with agents of the Cloak of Defiance, exposing surveillance, deception, and covert manipulation within the city. A violent confrontation revealed that the Cloak had been listening, watching, and preparing for escalation. In the aftermath, the party gained powerful items but also became persons of interest. By session's end, it was clear that neutrality was becoming harder to maintain.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 10 - The Sewers
sessionSummaries.summary: Returning underground, the party infiltrated deeper sections of the sewer network to disrupt cult operations directly. They uncovered hidden chambers, ritual spaces, and access points tied to grave robbing routes. Their actions weakened the cult's infrastructure but increased their visibility. Emberstran's underbelly was no longer operating unnoticed.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 9 - Sides and the 3rd Piece
sessionSummaries.summary: The party worked to secure another fragment of a mysterious tablet while navigating growing faction pressure. Conflicting interests pushed them toward choosing allies, even as they attempted to remain neutral. By the end of the session, they obtained the third tablet piece, confirming it was central to the cult's goals. The tablet's importance was no longer in doubt.

audioLinks.label: Session 9 - Sides and the 3rd Piece Recording

sessionSummaries.title: Session 8 - Cemetery Mystery
sessionSummaries.summary: Investigating disturbances at the cemetery, the party uncovered evidence of systematic grave desecration tied to the same cult operating in the sewers. Their efforts revealed patterns rather than isolated crimes. The group realized the dead were being used as resources rather than victims. This discovery tightened the connection between cult activity and the city's deeper corruption.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 7 - Divine Waypoint
sessionSummaries.summary: The party focused on understanding the divine mechanism within the grove and what would be required to activate it. They confirmed the waypoint's limitations and prepared for future travel through it. Along the way, subtle signs suggested that greater forces were watching their progress. The session clarified that divine travel would demand commitment, not convenience.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 6 - Diverra's Grove
sessionSummaries.summary: Following divine signs and earlier visions, the party traveled to a sacred grove tied to Diverra. There they witnessed the land's slow recovery from blight and confirmed the presence of a dormant waypoint. The site hinted at ancient protections and restrictions tied to faith. This journey established a path forward that extended beyond Emberstran itself.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 5 - Lady Rook
sessionSummaries.summary: The party made direct contact with Lady Rook and learned more about her influence over trade, protection, and information in Emberstran. While she presented herself as an ally, her reach and methods raised serious concerns. The group gained insight into how deeply entwined commerce, crime, and politics had become. Trust became a matter of calculated risk rather than belief.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 4 - Beneath the City
sessionSummaries.summary: The party descended into Emberstran's sewers to pursue cult activity linked to the earlier disturbances. They uncovered hidden passages, strange symbols, and signs of coordinated movement beneath the streets. Their investigation confirmed that grave robbing and ritual behavior were actively underway. The sewers proved to be a central artery of the city's hidden war.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 3 - EGD
sessionSummaries.summary: Seeking legitimacy and leverage, the party navigated early political pressure and learned they were being quietly evaluated by powerful factions. They uncovered evidence that their recent successes had drawn the attention of organized forces within Emberstran. By the end of the session, the groundwork was laid for the group to be formally recognized, though the source of that authority remained unclear. The party realized that recognition could be as dangerous as it was useful.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 2 - Bad Wine
sessionSummaries.summary: The party followed leads connected to a tainted vineyard and a suspicious murder tied to Emberstran's social elite. During a staged performance at the Old Oak, a real killing took place, confirming that poison and deception were being used to mask larger crimes. The group safeguarded key witnesses and brought the victim to the temple for rites. These events reinforced the idea that corruption was embedded both above and below the city's surface.

sessionSummaries.title: Session 1 - Dock Troubles
sessionSummaries.summary: The party arrived in Emberstran seeking work and quickly became involved in an investigation along the docks after reports of violence and strange activity. What initially appeared to be a mundane disturbance revealed signs of something unnatural, including parasitic creatures and reanimated corpses. The party destroyed the threat and ensured the dead were properly burned, preventing further spread. This first job established their reputation and hinted that darker forces were already at work beneath the city.

name: Mad Mage
description: A campaign of arcane mysteries, unstable power, hidden agendas, and strange discoveries beneath the surface of Myrdae.

sessionSummaries.title: Dungeon of the Mad Mage
sessionSummaries.summary: This campaign currently presents as a Dungeon of the Mad Mage hub with player notes.

name: Souls of Destiny
description: A cult, poisoned wells, dark heart rituals, and unlikely heroes pulled into danger around Adsuren.

sessionSummaries.title: 09 - That's a Good Looking Dwarf
sessionSummaries.summary: The party investigated reports of a raging bulas near mines. After slaying the creature and trading its pelt for magic boots, Mollywop's alarm sent them to a fisherman's hut where a large creature erupted from the river during Market Day.

sessionSummaries.title: 08 - Devil Spawn
sessionSummaries.summary: The party plunged into the hidden cellar beneath the Adsuren Center determined to root out the Cult of the Immured and shatter their blood rituals before more townsfolk fell victim. They carved through chanting cultists in a gore-slicked chamber only to unmask the respected Intendant Eyovar Dunajor as the fiendish summoner Karranok. The interrupted rite summoned the succubus Aeolenne who promptly declared Zephyra her daughter, sparking a chaotic battle. Though the demoness escaped, the party dismantled the cult, delivered justice to its traitor, and emerged victorious with treasures, revelations, and fresh resolve to press on into the mines.

sessionSummaries.title: 07 - A Solid Plan
sessionSummaries.summary: The party aimed to raid the cellar beneath Adsuren Center, their strongest lead on the cult, while deflecting blame for the missing town taxes. Esylla found Mollywop oddly exhausted. They planted stolen gold in Ganon's boarding house to get the guards off their trail. They descended into the cellar that night, proving it to be the hideout they were hoping to find. They found cultists and evidence of their rituals using hearts and black salt. Descending deeper and disposing of more cultists, the session ended at a set of double doors deep beneath Adsuren.

sessionSummaries.title: 06 - Who Took It?
sessionSummaries.summary: The party set out from Mollywop's tower determined to uncover the location of the rituals plaguing Adsuren and this Summoner Karranock. The heart-harvesting rites were taking place somewhere deeper in town. The day unfolded with tavern intrigue, dockside questioning, and a tense search of Ganon's rented room that revealed twitching rat heads and precise instructions for creating black salt from sentient hearts. Kenton added his own complications by quietly lifting tax pouches from the Adsuren Center, an act that quickly turned the town's Market Day preparations into a flurry of guarded suspicion. By session's end the Souls of Destiny found themselves chasing a shadowy cult while trying not to trip over the fresh chaos one of their own had sown.

sessionSummaries.title: 05 - Thugs & Cultists
sessionSummaries.summary: The party set out to track the shadowy cult responsible for poisoning Adsuren's well. Most of the group interrogated the local tanner and battled his cultist apprentice Ganon. However, Kenton was ambushed in an alley by thugs led by Sir Rodolf Norran from Gibuldon. Rodolf left behind a healing potion marked "Ryvennra" after Kenton fell. The party then consulted Mollywop the Magnificent, who identified the cult's lesser fiendish amulet and unholy black salt, and used Speak with Dead to learn that Summoner Karranock leads the group and their outdoor rituals occur somewhere deeper in Adsuren. Now aware that an imp may have alerted the cult, the heroes must carefully flush out their hidden enemies without alarming the village, while also bracing for Rodolf's next move.

sessionSummaries.title: 04 - Putrid
sessionSummaries.summary: Returning to Adsuren, the group sought answers about the growing corruption tied to stolen hearts and the ritual they had uncovered in the swamp. After consulting Mollywop, they learned the heart was human, bound by necromancy and fiendish magic, and likely created by a hidden group operating within the town itself. Investigating the graveyard, they confirmed their fears when an unearthed body missing its heart rose as an undead attacker. After destroying it, they were working with the early signs of a cult working in secret among Adsuren's people. Spreading out for more information led to an untimely attack upon Kenton, with the party unaware.

sessionSummaries.title: 03 - Fungipheliac Using Kinfolk
sessionSummaries.summary: The party sought to uncover what had happened to the missing Sapcutt family after being betrayed and trapped beneath their home. Fighting through fungal undead and deadly traps, they discovered the house itself was sustained by a sentient corruption born from something pulled out of the swamp. By rescuing Lobella and learning the grim bargain her mother had made, the party chose to save the living rather than destroy everything in their path. They escaped into the night with the surviving Sapcutts, knowing the true source of the corruption still lurked somewhere beyond the house.

sessionSummaries.title: 02 - Mollywop to Sapcutt
sessionSummaries.summary: After saving Adsuren's well, the party sought to understand the darker magic behind the corruption and welcomed a new companion, Lila, into their ranks. They destroyed the tainted heart under holy blessing and set out to consult Mollywop the Magnificent for deeper insight. In exchange for her help, they agreed to investigate the disappearance of the Sapcutt family and traveled into the swampy lowlands beyond the river. What began as a warm welcome at the Sapcutt ranch ended in betrayal, as the party was drugged during dinner and dropped through a hidden pit beneath the house.

sessionSummaries.title: 01 - We Meet
sessionSummaries.summary: In their first meeting in Adsuren, the newly formed party was drawn together by a sudden crisis when a villager collapsed after drinking from the town well. What began as a chance encounter quickly became a shared purpose as they set out to discover the source of the poisoning threatening the village. Their investigation led them beneath the settlement, where they fought through restless undead and uncovered a corrupted underground stream. By destroying the unnatural growth tainting the water and recovering its disturbing remnants, the party saved the well and prevented further harm, while realizing their brief alliance had become something more permanent.

name: The Silent Vanguard
description: Secret agents assembled by Master Thorne investigate outbreaks, conspiracies, and the ominous phrase The Long Night.

sessionSummaries.title: 02 - A Town Full of Zombies and a Mysterious Note
sessionSummaries.summary: The party survived the zombie outbreak in Brinecross, escaped the general store and local house, found Harold at the stable, recovered the information and an ivory/statue clue, learned the phrase/location The Long Night, escaped town on horses, returned to Tratta, reported the zombie outbreak, returned a postal horse and mail, leveled up, and met with Thorin on the ship to discuss the clues.

sessionSummaries.title: 01 - We Meet
sessionSummaries.summary: The party's first meeting took place in Tratta, a bustling sandstone harbor city watched over by a wizard's tower and split between public spectacle, dockside crime, hidden tunnels, thieves, spies, and darker powers. Master Thorne gathered six adventurers in a plain canvas tent near the Lower Dock markets, using the noise and cookstalls as cover.
